description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a tan fuselage and wings, featuring roundels on the upper surfaces of both wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The airplane has a dual-wing configuration with struts and wires connecting the upper and lower wings, providing structural support. The nose houses a visible engine and a two-bladed propeller, with the cockpit situated just behind the engine, likely open and exposed. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer with a split rudder and elevator. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, typical of early aircraft designs. The overall construction appears to be primarily wood and fabric, with some metal components for structural integrity.
description: A tan, vintage biplane with roundels on the wings, exposed engine and propeller, and fixed landing gear. Features dual wings with struts and an open cockpit behind the engine.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, roundels, exposed engine, and propeller, with the cockpit just behind the engine.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the high-wing biplane structure, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear with a tail skid.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly with a single vertical stabilizer and split horizontal stabilizer, as well as the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the biplane's wing struts, cockpit placement, and the tail skid under the rear fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane with straight, non-swept wings connected by vertical struts and wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer; split rudder and elevator.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located directly behind the engine; likely single-seat with no visible canopy.
